world.setting: A large country, young by other standards, bridging a continent from east to west. Encompassing vast plains, mountain ranges, deserts, mighty forests... A powerful alliance of city states making the "united dominions" much like the united states, but low tech (medieval) and low magic (game of thrones)
world.history: the united dominions have grown complacent and forgotten the principles they fought for. it was not their fault, their forebears were so successful in overcoming evils, they raised a generation who never knew of them. And that generation raised a new generation, who had no way to remember the grifts and tricks possible - they thought the old ways were the best as they had never experienced the horrors of e.g. poor dental care, low hygiene, corruption at every level, favouritism, cronyism, foreign interests actively seeking their downfall... they thought of them as issues other countries had, not them, never them... An age of prosperity, with a worm eating, growing in the centre. They had fought several wars with low impact. They were proud and thought it justified.
world.rules: Each city state has some autonomy, but rules under the central government, with representation. Magic is extremely rare, magical creatures are extrene
world.other: Perverts and cheats have always existed, but here they have found how to go undetected for too long. One after another, they are exposed and brought to light, but others are still operating in the shadows, learning and improving their camouflage.

style.tone: Gritty, realistic, brutal at times, but frequently holding up some fragment of the noble nature people are capable of.
style.voice: Third person of Gonad, with some chapters from  the perspective of the antagonist, Gonad Goldrump, with other chapters from the perspective of his enemy of the day.
style.vocabulary: Keep it concise, don't indulge in puffery - if a fact or event is mundane, feel free to describe it as such.

plot.goals: A stupid, vainglorious man born into wealth, let's call him Gonad Goldrump, learns that he can bully, manipulate and ultimately betray all who trust him despite being a shambling halfwit. He builds a small empire this way, but never changes his strategy or grows in any way. This works while he can operate in the shadows, concealing his actions from the wider world, finding new victims easily. However, he rises to a public position and is exposed, ruining the economy and international relationships for years to come. He does not accept responsibility and learns nothing, having long ago bought into his own myth.
plot.conflicts: On one side, a heroic, honourable society. The institutions rely on the assumption of decency. Our antagonist Gonad seeks to rip it all apart for his own gain. Defying all, he bulls his way through institutions and enterprises, twisting the law to his own ends.
plot.stakes: A mighty empire, benevolent and concerned with the betterment of all society, peace and progress. Will it be brought low by this figurehead of debasement and privilege?
plot.twists: The very people who he seeks to disenfranchise are confused and support him, due to his charisma and lies. Then the laws enable him (since he has managed to place his own people in power). It emerges expert assistance and funding was given to his chief advisers, from foreign powers intent on destroying those very people. Few people can believe anyone would be so blinkered, so blind. The evidence is not believed. The harmful effects are dismissed until... is it too late? Perhaps not, maybe an underdog can rise up and show a better way... many local grassroots efforts coordinate and combine, putting aside differences (after a struggle, with varying degrees of success) and establish a new order, a new resistance to the overlord Gonad Goldrump.
plot.philosophy: Liars and cheaters can only go so far - if they reach beyond "safe" areas of exploitation, they will be found out and do irreparable damage to the world.
